history.content:
<thought>
I am facing right on the second row. The goal `O` is in the lower-right area, and the row below has open space. Moving forward would hit lava immediately, so I should turn downward first to navigate safely toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am near the left side of the map facing down. The goal `O` is in the lower-right area, so I should head right. Since I am facing down, I need to turn left to face right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the second row, facing right. The goal `O` is below and to the right, but the direct path to the right is blocked by lava, so I should move downward along the open left corridor first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am near the left side of the map facing down. The goal `O` is on the lower-right side, and there is open floor to move through. First I should turn to face right so I can head toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the second row, facing right. The cell ahead is lava, so I should not move forward. To head toward the goal near the bottom-right, I need to go downward first. Turning right will face me down.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the map facing down, and the goal `O` is in the lower-right area. The cell directly below me is open, so I should move forward to progress toward the goal while avoiding the lava columns.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am near the left side of the map and facing down. The goal `O` is far to the right and slightly lower, so I need to start by turning to face right and then move along the open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing right (`>`). The goal `O` is below and to the right, and the path to it appears open through the lower corridor. Since the cell directly ahead is a lava tile `L`, I should not move forward. I need to turn downward first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am near the left side of the map facing down. The goal `O` is on the lower right, and the safest open route is to move down until I can head right along the bottom open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am near the lower-left area and facing down. The goal `O` is to the right on the same row near the bottom. My immediate best move is to turn right so I can head toward the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>

</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT

<thought>
I am near the lower-left area facing down. The goal `O` is on the lower-right side, and the row above the goal is open. Since moving forward would hit a wall, I should first turn left to face right, then I can head toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the map facing right. The goal `O` is below and to the right, in the lower area. Moving forward now would hit lava, so I need to first go down toward the open corridor. Turning left will make me face down.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the left side of the map facing up (`^`). The goal `O` is on the lower right, and the path to the right along my row is blocked by lava at the next cell, so I should move upward? No, that is a wall. I need to navigate around the lava columns; the safest immediate move is to turn right so I can head east toward the open corridor when possible.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT

<thought>
I am on the left side of the map facing right. The path to the goal is mostly open on the lower row, but there is lava immediately ahead in my current row, so I should head downward first to avoid it. Turning left will orient me downward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>

<thought>

TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>

</thought>

<thought>
